thesis: SK hynix is the dominant global supplier of HBM (high-bandwidth memory) for AI accelerators, holding ~50-55% HBM market share and an estimated 65-70% of NVIDIA's HBM4 orders. Q1 2026 revenue hit a record KRW 52.6T (+198% YoY) with 72% operating margins. The multiyear NVIDIA supply pact and Yongin/M15X fab expansion provide structural visibility through 2027. Memory cyclicality remains the primary risk.

drivers: HBM4 demand sustains through 2027 with NVIDIA Rubin/Blackwell cycle
Samsung HBM4 delays extend SK hynix pricing power
DRAM industry supply discipline holds, raising margin floor
Yongin Cluster and M15X ramp on schedule

risks: DRAM oversupply returns in 2027 as capex catches up
Samsung closes HBM4 technology gap and wins back NVIDIA share
AI capex cycle peaks and hyperscalers cut orders
Memory prices collapse 30-40% in a downturn

base_case.assumptions: HBM growth moderates but remains strong through 2027. Standard DRAM pricing normalizes gradually. Operating margins compress from 70%+ to 40-50% range. P/E ~10-12x on normalized earnings.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — HBM revenue mix and margin sustainability
HBM4 mass production volume ramp confirmation
NVIDIA Rubin/Blackwell launch validates HBM4 demand trajectory
FY2026 full-year results and FY2027 capex guidance

key_risks: Memory cyclicality — current 70%+ operating margins are unsustainable historically
AI capex cut risk from hyperscaler demand slowdown
China export controls and geopolitical risk
KRW/USD currency risk for foreign investors
